_meta.source: EAR (15 CFR Parts 730–774); NIST AI RMF 1.0 (2023); ISO/IEC 27001:2022; ISO/IEC 42001:2023
_meta.citation: EAR: 15 CFR Parts 730–774 (BIS); NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0 (Jan. 2023), NIST AI 100-1; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Information Security Management; ISO/IEC 42001:2023 AI Management Systems
_meta.note: Internal, employer-specific, and private-sector AI governance frameworks (e.g. G42/ADNOC-style) are out of scope for this public demonstrator. Only publicly published frameworks are mapped here.
_meta.retrieved_by: Manual seed — run scripts/refresh_rules.py to update
_meta.notes: PLACEHOLDER — Framework versions and control IDs should be verified against current published standards. ISO standards require purchase for full text. NIST AI RMF is freely available at https://airc.nist.gov/. EAR controls verified at https://www.ecfr.gov/.

frameworks.name: Export Administration Regulations (EAR)
frameworks.issuing_authority: Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS), US Department of Commerce
frameworks.description: The EAR controls exports, reexports, and transfers (in-country) of dual-use items, software, and technology. Classification under the EAR determines whether a licence is needed and what licence exceptions may apply.

relevant_controls.title: Scope of the EAR — Subject to EAR determination
relevant_controls.description: Determines whether an item is 'subject to the EAR'. All US-origin items are subject to EAR. Foreign-produced items incorporating controlled US-origin content above de minimis thresholds may also be subject (FDPR analysis required).

relevant_controls.title: Control Policy — NS, AT, and other multilateral controls
relevant_controls.description: Sets out control policies for NS (national security), AT (anti-terrorism), and other control reasons. NS1 and AT1 columns on the BIS Country Chart trigger licence requirements for controlled items to listed countries.

relevant_controls.title: End-Use/End-User Controls (Part 744)
relevant_controls.description: Prohibits exports/reexports to specific prohibited end-uses (military end-use, WMD activities) and end-users (Entity List, MEU List, unverified parties). Applies regardless of ECCN classification.
relevant_controls.citation: 15 CFR §§ 744.1–744.24; Supplement No. 4 (Entity List); Supplement No. 7 (MEU List)

relevant_controls.title: Embargoes and Other Special Controls
relevant_controls.description: Country-specific restrictions implementing comprehensive or targeted embargoes. Russia/Belarus (§ 746.7/746.8), Iran (§ 746.4), North Korea (§ 746.5), Cuba (§ 746.2), Syria (§ 746.6), China advanced computing (§ 746.9).

relevant_controls.title: Licence Exception ENC — Encryption
relevant_controls.description: Authorises export of certain encryption items without a licence, subject to eligibility requirements (mass-market, key length, authorised use). Not available for E:1/E:2 destinations or Entity List parties.
relevant_controls.citation: 15 CFR § 740.17; Supplement No. 1 to Part 740 (Country Groups)

relevant_controls.title: Foreign Direct Product Rule (FDPR)
relevant_controls.description: Extends EAR jurisdiction to foreign-produced items that are the direct product of US-origin technology or software, or produced using US equipment or software, where the item meets certain criteria and is destined for certain parties or countries.

frameworks.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)
frameworks.issuing_authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), US Department of Commerce
frameworks.description: A voluntary framework for organisations to manage risks associated with AI systems throughout their lifecycle. Structured around four core functions: GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE. Not a regulatory requirement but increasingly referenced in procurement and risk management contexts.

relevant_controls.title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organisation related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks
relevant_controls.description: Organisations should establish policies for AI risk governance, including export control compliance for AI systems deployed internationally. AI systems with dual-use potential require documented risk governance.

relevant_controls.title: Organisational risk and benefits are understood with regard to external deployments
relevant_controls.description: AI systems intended for international deployment (including cloud/SaaS) should be mapped for export control exposure — including deemed-export risks, end-user screening requirements, and ECCN classification.

relevant_controls.title: AI risks and benefits are evaluated for impact and likelihood
relevant_controls.description: Risk measurement should include regulatory/legal dimensions including export control compliance risk. High-performance AI systems face heightened classification risk under 3A090/4A090 controls.

relevant_controls.title: Risk treatments are prioritised and implemented
relevant_controls.description: Export control compliance actions (licence applications, end-user screening, access controls) should be integrated into AI risk management plans. Fine-tuning capabilities and model weight access require specific access controls.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 — Information Security Management Systems
frameworks.issuing_authority: International Organisation for Standardisation (ISO) / International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)
frameworks.description: The international standard for information security management systems (ISMS). Includes controls relevant to encryption, access management, and supplier security — all of which intersect with EAR cryptographic controls (5A002, 5D002) and deemed-export obligations.

relevant_controls.title: Use of Cryptography
relevant_controls.description: Organisations must implement a policy on the use of cryptographic controls. Export control review of cryptographic algorithms and key lengths is required under EAR 5A002/5D002. ENC licence exception eligibility depends on the specific cryptographic implementation.

relevant_controls.title: Information Security in Supplier Relationships
relevant_controls.description: Security controls in supplier relationships should include export control due diligence — particularly where suppliers or their sub-processors are located in controlled countries or are state-affiliated entities.

relevant_controls.title: Access Control
relevant_controls.description: Access controls must include nationality/country-of-origin verification for users of EAR-controlled software/platforms. SaaS/IaaS providers must implement controls preventing deemed exports to restricted nationals.

relevant_controls.title: Configuration Management
relevant_controls.description: Configuration management for AI systems includes maintaining records of model versions, training data provenance, and deployment configurations — supporting audit trail requirements for EAR compliance.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — Artificial Intelligence Management Systems
frameworks.issuing_authority: International Organisation for Standardisation (ISO) / International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)
frameworks.description: The first international standard for AI management systems. Establishes requirements for responsible development, deployment, and monitoring of AI. Directly relevant to AI systems subject to EAR controls, particularly those with dual-use potential.

relevant_controls.title: Actions to Address Risks and Opportunities
relevant_controls.description: Risk assessment for AI systems should include export control exposure: ECCN classification risk, deemed-export risk, end-user screening obligations. AI systems with dual-use potential require heightened risk assessment documentation.

relevant_controls.title: AI System Impact Assessment
relevant_controls.description: AI impact assessments should include export control compliance dimensions — particularly for AI systems intended for international deployment. Systems involving advanced computing capabilities or model weights warrant ECCN classification review.

relevant_controls.title: Monitoring, Measurement, Analysis, and Evaluation
relevant_controls.description: AI management systems should monitor regulatory developments affecting AI governance, including BIS/OFAC regulatory changes that affect ECCN classification of AI items. Staleness of export control data snapshots should be tracked.

relevant_controls.title: Responsible Use of AI — Dual-Use Considerations
relevant_controls.description: AI systems designed or capable of dual-use applications (civilian and military/intelligence) require specific controls and documentation. Export to state-owned entities in controlled countries raises dual-use concerns requiring enhanced due diligence.
